title: Averages & Mixtures
learning_outcomes: Describe structural characteristics of Averages & Mixtures.
Derive mathematical and programmatic bounds for Simple averages vs weighted averages, Alligation ratios calculations in mixtures, Replacement loops in liquids.

content_sections.title: Averages & Mixtures
content_sections.content:
### Arithmetic, Totients & Combinatorics
Quantitative Aptitude focuses on number relationships, algebraic systems, and probability calculations.

#### Euler's Totient Theorem
For coprimes $a$ and $n$, the remainder of $a^{\phi(n)} \pmod n$ is $1$, where $\phi(n)$ is Euler's totient (count of numbers coprime to $n$ up to $n$):
$$\phi(n) = n \prod_{p \mid n} \left(1 - \frac{1}{p}\right)$$
For primes, $\phi(p) = p - 1$, yielding Fermat's Little Theorem: $a^{p-1} \equiv 1 \pmod p$.

#### Product Consistency Constraint
If $A \times B = C$ and $C$ remains constant, a fractional increase in $A$ by $x/y$ requires a fractional decrease in $B$ by $\frac{x}{x + y}$ to maintain product consistency.

#### Stars-and-Bars Partitions
To distribute $n$ identical objects among $k$ distinct bins:
*   *Empty bins allowed:* $\binom{n + k - 1}{k - 1}$ partitions.
*   *No empty bins allowed:* $\binom{n - 1}{k - 1}$ partitions.

### Depth Analysis: Averages & Mixtures
In production configurations, we analyze how Simple averages vs weighted averages, Alligation ratios calculations in mixtures, Replacement loops in liquids affects performance parameters. We evaluate memory layouts and consensus thresholds.

callouts.content: A common mistake when analyzing Averages & Mixtures is ignoring latency bounds and boundary edge cases during concurrent executions.

callouts.title: Production Scaling for Averages & Mixtures
callouts.content: Production servers implement strict resource isolation and cache alignments to prevent performance regressions when running Averages & Mixtures loops.
